title: In-process notification check at tool batch boundaries
purpose: Active Norn agents never learn about new notifications (channel mentions, group messages, system alerts) unless they explicitly call meridian_messaging inbox. The Claude Runner path uses a PreToolUse shell hook that runs 'meridian message notify check' between every tool call. Norn needs an in-process equivalent that checks pending notifications at tool batch boundaries and injects them via InboundSender, without subprocess overhead. This is a new mechanism — the notification check infrastructure does not exist today for Norn sessions.
task: Add two missing service wrapper methods (get_pending_notifications, count_pending_notifications) to the messaging wrapper. Create a NotificationChecker that queries pending notifications after each tool batch completes and injects them as structured content via InboundSender with DeliveryMode::Steer. The checker is constructed per-session from ServiceContext and CallerContext member_id. Notifications are formatted as XML matching the existing notify check --xml output. Injected notifications are marked as delivered to prevent re-injection.

requirements.title: MessagingService notification wrapper methods
requirements.spec: WHEN a Norn session component needs to query pending notifications THE SYSTEM SHALL call MessagingService wrapper methods that delegate to the existing NotificationStore trait AND it SHALL NOT require direct storage trait access from outside the messaging service.
acceptance: get_pending_notifications(member_id: &Uuid, limit: Option<usize>) -> ServiceResult<Vec<Notification>> exists on MessagingService wrapper
count_pending_notifications(member_id: &Uuid) -> ServiceResult<usize> exists on MessagingService wrapper
Both delegate to NotificationStore trait methods at crates/meridian-storage-pg/src/stores/notification_store.rs (lines 163-188 and 234-247)
Both return ServiceResult, not StorageResult — error mapping at the wrapper boundary

requirements.title: NotificationChecker constructed per-session
requirements.spec: WHEN a Norn member-facing session is constructed in spawn_norn_loop THE SYSTEM SHALL create a NotificationChecker that holds a reference to the MessagingService and the caller's member_id AND the NotificationChecker SHALL be accessible at tool batch drain points in the agent loop.
acceptance: NotificationChecker struct holds Arc<MessagingService> (or the service wrapper) and member_id: Uuid
NotificationChecker has async fn check(&self) -> Vec<Notification> that calls get_pending_notifications with limit 20
NotificationChecker has fn format_xml(&self, notifications: &[Notification]) -> String that produces XML matching the existing notify check --xml output format
NotificationChecker is constructed in spawn_norn_loop (service.rs) using ServiceContext and CallerContext.member_id
NotificationChecker is published as an Arc extension on ToolContext via AgentBuilder::extension(), making it accessible during tool dispatch

requirements.title: Post-tool-batch notification injection
requirements.spec: WHEN a tool batch completes in a Norn session THE SYSTEM SHALL check for pending notifications via NotificationChecker AND WHEN notifications exist THE SYSTEM SHALL format them and inject as a ChannelMessage with DeliveryMode::Steer via InboundSender AND it SHALL NOT inject when zero notifications are pending.
acceptance: Notification check runs after execute_tool_batch returns, at or near the existing drain point (runner.rs around line 709)
Pending notifications are formatted as XML and sent as a ChannelMessage { author: 'system', content: xml, delivery: DeliveryMode::Steer, timestamp: Utc::now() }
The agent receives notification content at the next drain_and_partition call, before the LLM turn
No ChannelMessage is sent when zero notifications are pending
The check does not run during the first tool batch after wake — build_wake_prompt already delivers pending notifications in the wake prompt

requirements.title: Mark injected notifications as delivered
requirements.spec: WHEN notifications are injected into the agent's inbound channel THE SYSTEM SHALL mark them as delivered so they are not re-injected on the next tool batch AND it SHALL NOT mark notifications if the InboundSender.send() call fails.
acceptance: After successful InboundSender.send(), the injected notification IDs are passed to mark_notifications_delivered on the MessagingService
Failed send (channel full or closed) skips the mark — notifications remain pending for next check
Notifications are not double-injected across consecutive tool batches within the same session

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the InboundChannel or InboundSender types in the norn crate — uses the existing send API
SHALL NOT remove or modify the CLI notify check hook — it continues to work for Claude Runner sessions
SHALL NOT poll notifications on a timer — runs only at tool batch boundaries
SHALL NOT inject notifications during the first tool batch of a wake session
verification: cargo check --workspace passes with the new notification_checker module
NotificationChecker.check() returns pending notifications from the service layer
Notifications injected via InboundSender appear in the agent's next drain_and_partition result
Injected notifications are marked as delivered and not re-injected on subsequent tool batches
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
